decks.notes: This deck aims to control the game by using the Fires of Invention enchantment to cast spells for free. By utilizing cards like Witch's Oven, Cauldron Familiar, and Scavenger's Talent to generate value, along with removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, the deck aims to outgrind opponents. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Ygra, Eater of All and Badgermole Cub provide a win condition, while cards like Witherbloom Charm offer versatility. The deck also includes lands like Darkbore Pathway and Traverse the Ulvenwald to ensure consistent mana fixing and card advantage.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's hand and board state with cards like Thoughtseize, Duress, and Abrupt Decay, while also ramping up with cards like Blooming Marsh and Llanowar Wastes. The key strategy revolves around controlling the game through removal spells and disruption, eventually overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's game plan, this deck looks to grind out wins in a slower, more controlling fashion.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful board presence through the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II. By utilizing cards like Esika's Chariot and Overlord of the Balemurk, the deck aims to generate value and overwhelm the opponent with its creatures. Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command provide disruption and control elements, while cards like Collective Brutality and Cache Grab offer versatility in dealing with different situations. The deck also includes some mana fixing and removal options to ensure a smooth gameplay experience.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly establish board presence and deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, along with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, this deck aims to apply pressure early on. Cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Riverpyre Verge help to increase damage output, while spells like Academic Dispute and Consider provide card advantage and dig for key pieces. The deck also has some evasion and flexibility with cards like Vivi Ornitier and Boomerang Basics to help maintain tempo and control the game.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate powerful synergies and create a dominant board state. By using cards like Fleeting Spirit and Guardian of New Benalia to support the combo, the deck aims to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy while building towards its win condition. With a mix of removal, card draw, and mana fixing, this deck is designed to be versatile and adaptable to different matchups, ultimately aiming to overwhelm the opponent with its combo potential.

This deck focuses on building a fast and aggressive board presence using low-cost creatures and mana acceleration cards like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with early creature threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. Collected Company allows for card advantage and the potential to rapidly refill the board with creatures. The deck also includes some resilience with Enduring Innocence and Ouroboroid to protect key creatures and maintain pressure on the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize a combination of efficient creature cards and ramp cards to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures. The deck aims to establish an early board presence using cards like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. Collected Company is used to generate card advantage and find key creatures, while Skyclave Apparition and Elspeth, Storm Slayer provide removal options. Overall, the deck aims to pressure the opponent and close out the game quickly with aggressive creatures.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through disruption and removal while slowly building up resources and board presence. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, remove key threats with Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, and generate value with cards like Culling Ritual and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The inclusion of cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Boseiju, Who Endures provide additional control and resilience against opposing strategies, making it a powerful and versatile control deck in the format.

This deck focuses on using a combination of disruption and powerful threats to control the game. It includes cards like Thoughseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while also utilizing powerful creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Badgermole Cub to apply pressure. Additionally, the deck includes removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push to deal with opposing threats. The deck aims to grind out a win through resource denial and efficient creature combat, making it difficult for opponents to execute their game plan effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and spells. By using efficient card draw and burn spells like Burst Lightning, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while maintaining card advantage. Using cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, the deck can deal consistent damage and generate additional value from spells. Overall, the deck is designed to be fast-paced and proactive, aiming to take control of the game early on and push for a quick victory.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with quick and efficient creatures while also utilizing spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By combining aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, this deck aims to deal damage early and often, keeping the pressure on the opponent. Additionally, cards like Academic Dispute and Flow State provide card draw and versatile options for further flexibility in gameplay. Overall, this deck focuses on fast-paced aggression and strategic spellcasting to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively attack the opponent using low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while also supporting them with instant spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage. The deck aims to maintain a fast tempo and apply pressure to the opponent, with some card draw and spell manipulation options to ensure a consistent flow of threats. The deck also includes capabilities for controlling opponent's board with cards like Cori-Steel Cutter and Vivi Ornitier.

This deck focuses on using Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly in order to cast powerful spells like Ultimatum','','');">Emergent Ultimatum and Omniscience. The deck also includes cards like Hidden Strings and Sylvan Scrying to help find and untap Lotus Field, as well as cards like Peer into the Abyss and Formidable Speaker to draw cards and gain advantage. The goal is to set up a powerful combo that can win the game in a single turn, overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of creature removal, hand disruption, and powerful control elements to dominate the game. It aims to control the board with efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Thoughtseize. The creatures in the deck provide additional threats and can help maintain board presence. The deck is designed to grind out opponents through attrition, ultimately overwhelming them with its powerful control elements and resilient creatures.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost spells and creatures. It includes a mix of burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to maintain board control, along with efficient creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to deal consistent damage. The deck also features cards like Boomerang Basics and Riverpyre Verge to ensure mana efficiency and card advantage, allowing the player to keep up the pressure throughout the game. Overall, the strategy revolves around maintaining a fast-paced and aggressive tempo to secure a win.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly and efficiently dealing damage to the opponent with low-cost spells like Fiery Impulse and Cori-Steel Cutter, supported by creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear. The deck also includes card draw and selection through spells like Sleight of Hand and Consider to maintain momentum. The inclusion of cards like Reckless Rage and Boomerang Basics offer some control options while maintaining pressure on the opponent. The key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive spells and creatures while maintaining tempo and card advantage.
